with open("/etc/profile","r") as f:
    lines = f.readlines()
    new_lines = []
    for line in lines:
        new_lines.append(line)
        if line.strip().startswith("export PATH"):

execution_details.stdout:
# /etc/profile

# System wide environment and startup programs, for login setup
# Functions and aliases go in /etc/bashrc

# It's NOT a good idea to change this file unless you know what you
# are doing. It's much better to create a custom.sh shell script in
# /etc/profile.d/ to make custom changes to your environment, as this
# will prevent the need for merging in future updates.

pathmunge () {
    case ":${PATH}:" in
        *:"$1":*)
            ;;
        *)
            if [ "$2" = "after" ] ; then
                PATH=$PATH:$1
            else
                PATH=$1:$PATH
            fi
    esac
}


if [ -x /usr/bin/id ]; then
    if [ -z "$EUID" ]; then
        # ksh workaround
        EUID=`/usr/bin/id -u`
        UID=`/usr/bin/id -ru`
    fi
    USER="`/usr/bin/id -un`"
    LOGNAME=$USER
    MAIL="/var/spool/mail/$USER"
fi

# Path manipulation
if [ "$EUID" = "0" ]; then
    pathmunge /usr/sbin
    pathmunge /usr/local/sbin
else
    pathmunge /usr/local/sbin after
    pathmunge /usr/sbin after
fi

HOSTNAME=$(/usr/bin/hostnamectl --transient 2>/dev/null) || \
HOSTNAME=$(/usr/bin/hostname 2>/dev/null) || \
HOSTNAME=$(/usr/bin/uname -n)

HISTSIZE=1000
if [ "$HISTCONTROL" = "ignorespace" ] ; then
    export HISTCONTROL=ignoreboth
else
    export HISTCONTROL=ignoredups
fi

export PATH USER LOGNAME MAIL HOSTNAME HISTSIZE HISTCONTROL

for i in /etc/profile.d/*.sh /etc/profile.d/sh.local ; do
    if [ -r "$i" ]; then
        if [ "${-#*i}" != "$-" ]; then 
            . "$i"
        else
            . "$i" >/dev/null
        fi
    fi
done

unset i
unset -f pathmunge

if [ -n "${BASH_VERSION-}" ] ; then
        if [ -f /etc/bashrc ] ; then
                # Bash login shells run only /etc/profile
                # Bash non-login shells run only /etc/bashrc
                # Check for double sourcing is done in /etc/bashrc.
                . /etc/bashrc
       fi
fi

execution_details.stdout:
# /etc/profile

# System wide environment and startup programs, for login setup
# Functions and aliases go in /etc/bashrc

# It's NOT a good idea to change this file unless you know what you
# are doing. It's much better to create a custom.sh shell script in
# /etc/profile.d/ to make custom changes to your environment, as this
# will prevent the need for merging in future updates.

pathmunge () {
    case \":${PATH}:\" in
        *:\"$1\":*)\n            ;;\n        *)\n            if [ \"$2\" = \"after\" ] ; then
                PATH=$PATH:$1
            else
                PATH=$1:$PATH
            fi
    esac
}



if [ -x /usr/bin/id ]; then
    if [ -z \"$EUID\" ]; then
        # ksh workaround
        EUID=`/usr/bin/id -u`
        UID=`/usr/bin/id -ru`
    fi
    USER=\"`/usr/bin/id -un`\"\n    LOGNAME=$USER\n    MAIL=\"/var/spool/mail/$USER\"\nfi



# Path manipulation
if [ \"$EUID\" = \"0\" ]; then
    pathmunge /usr/sbin
    pathmunge /usr/local/sbin
else
    pathmunge /usr/local/sbin after
    pathmunge /usr/sbin after
fi



HOSTNAME=$(/usr/bin/hostnamectl --transient 2>/dev/null) || \\
HOSTNAME=$(/usr/bin/hostname 2>/dev/null) || \\
HOSTNAME=$(/usr/bin/uname -n)



HISTSIZE=1000
if [ \"$HISTCONTROL\" = \"ignorespace\" ] ; then
    export HISTCONTROL=ignoreboth
else
    export HISTCONTROL=ignoredups
fi
\nexport PATH USER LOGNAME MAIL HOSTNAME HISTSIZE HISTCONTROL
umask 022

for i in /etc/profile.d/*.sh /etc/profile.d/sh.local ; do
    if [ -r \"$i\" ]; then
        if [ \"${-#*i}\" != \"$-\" ]; then \n            . \"$i\"\n        else\n            . \"$i\" >/dev/null\n        fi
    fi
done

unset i
unset -f pathmunge

if [ -n \"${BASH_VERSION-}\" ] ; then
    if [ -f /etc/bashrc ] ; then
            # Bash login shells run only /etc/profile
            # Bash non-login shells run only /etc/bashrc
            # Check for double sourcing is done in /etc/bashrc.
            . /etc/bashrc
       fi
fi

python3 -c "
import sys
content = '''# /etc/profile

# System wide environment and startup programs, for login setup
# Functions and aliases go in /etc/bashrc

# It's NOT a good idea to change this file unless you know what you
# are doing. It's much better to create a custom.sh shell script in
# /etc/profile.d/ to make custom changes to your environment, as this
# will prevent the need for merging in future updates.

pathmunge () {
    case \"${PATH}:\" in
        *:\"$1\":*)
            ;;
        *)
            if [ \"$2\" = \"after\" ] ; then
                PATH=$PATH:$1
            else
                PATH=$1:$PATH
            fi
    esac
}



if [ -x /usr/bin/id ]; then
    if [ -z \"$EUID\" ]; then
        # ksh workaround
        EUID=`/usr/bin/id -u`
        UID=`/usr/bin/id -ru`
    fi
    USER=\"`/usr/bin/id -un`\"
    LOGNAME=$USER
    MAIL=\"/var/spool/mail/$USER\"
fi



# Path manipulation
if [ \"$EUID\" = \"0\" ]; then
    pathmunge /usr/sbin
    pathmunge /usr/local/sbin
else
    pathmunge /usr/local/sbin after

We can use python to read the file, modify, and write back.

Let's do a python script that reads the file, splits lines, inserts umask line after the export line, then writes back.

We'll do:

run_cmd with command: python3 -c "
import sys
with open('/etc/profile','r') as f:
    lines = f.readlines()
new_lines = []
for line in lines:
    new_lines.append(line)
    if line.strip().startswith('export PATH'):
        new_lines.append(line)
        new_lines.append('umask 022\n')
    with open('/etc/profile','w') as f:
    f.writelines(new_lines)
"
